sections.paragraph:
to contract oneself, to draw oneself together (cogn. to גָּבָא, syn. גָּבַן), specially-

(1) to draw up the feet, to sit with the feet drawn up, Zep 1:12 (compare Jer 48:11).

(2) to curdle, to coagulate as milk (see Hiph.); poet. of the water of the sea, Exo 15:8 [speaking of a literal miracle].

[“Niphal, to be contracted, withdrawn, Zec 14:6, כתיב.”]

Hiphil, causat. of No. 2, Job 10:10. (Talmud. id. Arab. and Syr. قَفَّ, ܩܰܦ id.) Hence-
